$comment: LifeSmart OmniPlatform — Firestore structure inferred from src/. Deploy firestore.rules with Firebase CLI. Rules are authoritative for access control.

collections.description: Primary user profile; created via ensureUserDoc (userUtils.js).
Grouped tool navigation config; read by useToolConfig, written by AdminToolConfig / saveToolConfig.

fields.groups: array of { id, label, order, tools: [...] }

collections.description: Admin system settings blob from AdminSystemSettings.
Access codes; doc id is the code string. Managed in AdminLoginCodes.

fields.lastUsedBy: string uid (optional; referenced in admin UI)

collections.description: Shared simulation run metadata. simulationDocId pattern: 'Simulation {n}'. Written by SimulationControls.
Cached market data (initFirebase.js). No current callers besides helpers.
Past simulations list in PastSimulations.js. No writes found in codebase; schema from UI expectations.
Per-user preferences. Collection id equals Auth uid; document id 'Settings'. SettingsScreen.js.
Mirror profile doc updated with Users from EditModal (admin).
collections.fields: same shape as admin edit payload (firstName, lastName, email, isActive, admin, developer, user)

collections.description: School quiz team scores; FinancialQuiz.js. Team doc id is team name.

fields.name: string
fields.taskScores: map of task index to score

fields.finalValues: array of { name, equity, bonds, realestate, commodities, other }

collections.description: Referenced by AdminDatabaseManagement stats/purge only; schema not defined in app.

securityNotes: Elevated privilege in rules matches AdminHome: admin, developer, isAdmin, or role == 'admin'.
Self-service user updates cannot change privilegedUserFields (see firestore.rules).
Default deny: paths not matched by firestore.rules are inaccessible.
Admin routes in React are not a security boundary; rules enforce access.
Consider Cloud Functions for Login Codes validation and stock writes if you want zero client write surface.
